title: Diarthron ramosissimum

This family is part of the larger clade of flowering plants, known as the angiosperms. The Melastomataceae family consists of over 150 species, with a wide range of habitats and geographical distributions. The classification of Diarthron ramosissimum places it within the genus Diarthron, which is a relatively small and poorly studied group of plants. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and relationships of this species within the larger context of plant systematics.
